@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","title":"Audit and expand Jargon component terms","description":"# Task: Audit and Expand Jargon Component Terms ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description The Jargon component provides tooltip definitions for technical terms, but coverage is incomplete and some definitions are too technical. Need a comprehensive audit. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/components/jargon.tsx ### Terms to audit/add: - VPS - Virtual Private Server (already exists, verify quality) - SSH - Secure Shell (verify quality) - Terminal - text interface (verify quality) - Command line - same as terminal (alias?) - Root - superuser/admin account - sudo - run as superuser - Ubuntu user - regular user account - tmux - terminal multiplexer - Session - persistent terminal environment - Repository/Repo - code storage folder - Git - version control system - API key - authentication token - Environment variable - system setting - Port - network endpoint - IP address - network address - zsh - shell program - Bash - shell program ### Quality criteria for definitions: - No jargon in the definition itself - One sentence maximum for tooltip - Relatable analogy if possible - Consistent tone ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms listed above have definitions - [ ] Definitions are beginner-friendly (no technical jargon) - [ ] Tooltip text fits comfortably (not too long) - [ ] All wizard pages use Jargon wrappers appropriately - [ ] Mobile tooltip experience is good (tap to show) ## UI/UX Notes - Tooltips should be quick reads (2-3 seconds) - Consider linking to glossary for longer explanations - Test on mobile — touch targets and positioning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:44:46.425151-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","close_reason":"Added 4 new beginner-friendly jargon terms: session, api-key, environment-variable, clone. All terms include short/long descriptions, analogies, and 'why we use it' explanations. Checked for and avoided duplicates with existing terms."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-37sa","title":"Add p10k wizard handling instructions","description":"# Task: Add Powerlevel10k Wizard Handling ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np) ## Description On first zsh login, Powerlevel10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing for beginners. They don't know what it's asking or if they should configure it. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/reconnect-ubuntu/page.tsx Add a GuideTip or AlertCard: \`\`\`tsx Prompt customization wizard? When you first connect, you might see a "Powerlevel10k configuration wizard" asking about fonts and prompt style. You can:
  • Press q to quit and use defaults (recommended for now)
  • Or go through it if you want to customize your prompt appearance

You can always run p10k configure later to customize.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Warns about possible p10k wizard - [ ] Clear instruction to press 'q' to skip - [ ] Mentions ability to configure later - [ ] Non-scary tone (it's a feature, not a problem) ## UI/UX Notes - Use GuideTip (not warning) — this is informational - Keep it brief; most users will just press q - "Recommended for now" guides beginners to the simpler path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:43:03.190685-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","close_reason":"Added a friendly tip about the p10k wizard (press q to skip; p10k configure later)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ci","title":"Fix acfs doctor --json output (pure JSON + correct counts)","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json currently prints section headings before JSON, doesn’t increment pass/warn/fail counters in JSON mode, and doesn’t escape strings. Make --json emit only valid JSON on stdout with correct summary counts (and handle missing /etc/os-release).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:44:19.646755-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json to emit pure valid JSON (no headings), increment counters in JSON mode, and JSON-escape strings; also handles missing /etc/os-release."} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj","title":"[EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS","description":"# Epic: Authentication Flow for Headless VPS ## Problem Statement When users run auth commands like `claude` or `gh auth login` on their VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But the VPS has no browser! The tool outputs a URL that the user must manually open on their laptop. This flow is: 1. Not explained anywhere in the wizard 2. Confusing when it says "Opening browser..." but nothing happens 3. Even more confusing if xdg-open is installed and fails silently Additionally, the accounts page shows too many services at once, and subscription requirements (Claude Pro, ChatGPT Pro) aren't prominent enough. ## Background & Reasoning OAuth flows in CLI tools assume a local machine with a browser. Headless servers require the "device flow" where a URL is displayed for manual opening. Users need to: 1. See the URL in the terminal 2. Copy it 3. Paste into their laptop's browser 4. Complete the OAuth flow 5. Return to terminal This is standard for developers but completely foreign to beginners. Without guidance, they'll think the auth failed. ## User Story As a beginner authenticating services on my VPS, I want to understand the device authentication flow, So that I can successfully connect my accounts without confusion. ## Success Criteria 1. User understands that VPS has no browser 2. User knows to copy the URL and paste in laptop browser 3. User knows to return to terminal after browser auth completes 4. User understands which services need paid subscriptions 5. User feels safe skipping non-essential services ## Scope - Add "Headless Authentication" explanation to status-check page - Enhance accounts page to de-emphasize optional services - Add subscription requirement warnings prominently - Add "What happens if I skip?" reassurance - Consider reordering: Essential only by default, expand for more ## Dependencies - Epic D: First SSH Connection (user must be connected to VPS) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Use AlertCard for the headless auth explanation - Subscription warnings should use distinct visual treatment - "Skip" button needs reassurance text - Mobile: auth command copy must be easy ## Technical Approach 1. Add prominent AlertCard to status-check page explaining device flow 2. Add "Copy URL, paste in laptop browser" instructions 3. Enhance service cards with subscription badges 4. Default-collapse Recommended and Optional tiers 5. Add reassurance text near skip button ## Estimated Effort Medium (2 wizard page updates) ## Priority Justification P1 (High) - Without this, users cannot authenticate and cannot use the tools they just installed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:35:19.713077-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:35:19.713077-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:37:54.358146-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj","title":"[EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS","description":"# Epic: Authentication Flow for Headless VPS ## Problem Statement When users run auth commands like `claude` or `gh auth login` on their VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But the VPS has no browser! The tool outputs a URL that the user must manually open on their laptop. This flow is: 1. Not explained anywhere in the wizard 2. Confusing when it says "Opening browser..." but nothing happens 3. Even more confusing if xdg-open is installed and fails silently Additionally, the accounts page shows too many services at once, and subscription requirements (Claude Pro, ChatGPT Pro) aren't prominent enough. ## Background & Reasoning OAuth flows in CLI tools assume a local machine with a browser. Headless servers require the "device flow" where a URL is displayed for manual opening. Users need to: 1. See the URL in the terminal 2. Copy it 3. Paste into their laptop's browser 4. Complete the OAuth flow 5. Return to terminal This is standard for developers but completely foreign to beginners. Without guidance, they'll think the auth failed. ## User Story As a beginner authenticating services on my VPS, I want to understand the device authentication flow, So that I can successfully connect my accounts without confusion. ## Success Criteria 1. User understands that VPS has no browser 2. User knows to copy the URL and paste in laptop browser 3. User knows to return to terminal after browser auth completes 4. User understands which services need paid subscriptions 5. User feels safe skipping non-essential services ## Scope - Add "Headless Authentication" explanation to status-check page - Enhance accounts page to de-emphasize optional services - Add subscription requirement warnings prominently - Add "What happens if I skip?" reassurance - Consider reordering: Essential only by default, expand for more ## Dependencies - Epic D: First SSH Connection (user must be connected to VPS) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Use AlertCard for the headless auth explanation - Subscription warnings should use distinct visual treatment - "Skip" button needs reassurance text - Mobile: auth command copy must be easy ## Technical Approach 1. Add prominent AlertCard to status-check page explaining device flow 2. Add "Copy URL, paste in laptop browser" instructions 3. Enhance service cards with subscription badges 4. Default-collapse Recommended and Optional tiers 5. Add reassurance text near skip button ## Estimated Effort Medium (2 wizard page updates) ## Priority Justification P1 (High) - Without this, users cannot authenticate and cannot use the tools they just installed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:35:19.713077-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:50:10.074975-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:50:10.074975-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented across multiple beads: headless auth explanation in status-check (dmff), subscription badges on accounts page (2gs8), collapsible tiers with essential defaultOpen, skip button with reassurance text. All success criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:37:54.358146-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ht","title":"Implement cloud CLI updates (vercel, wrangler, supabase, fly)","description":"## What Implement the 'cloud' category for acfs-update: - Vercel CLI: bun update -g vercel - Wrangler (Cloudflare): bun update -g wrangler - Supabase CLI: bun update -g supabase - Fly CLI: Reinstall via official installer (flyctl self-upgrade) - AWS CLI: Detect install method, update accordingly ## AWS CLI Handling (added) AWS CLI can be installed multiple ways: 1. apt-installed: Handled by system updates (apt upgrade) 2. pip-installed: pip install --upgrade awscli 3. Official installer: Re-run installer or use aws --update Detection: Check `which aws`, then `dpkg -s awscli` vs `pip show awscli` ## Technical Details - Most cloud CLIs are bun global packages - simple updates - Fly CLI has built-in: flyctl self-upgrade (or reinstall) - Cloud CLIs may have authentication state - preserve it ## Considerations - Only update tools that are installed - Skip tools without prompting if not present - For bun packages: bun update -g - Log version changes ## Success Criteria - [ ] All cloud CLIs update correctly - [ ] AWS CLI detected and updated properly - [ ] Skips uninstalled CLIs - [ ] Authentication preserved - [ ] Version changes logged","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:43.995628-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:23.18063-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:23.18063-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in scripts/lib/update.sh:update_cloud() - Wrangler, Supabase, Vercel via bun install -g @latest","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ht","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.514415-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3mp","title":"Implement runtime updates (bun, rust, uv, go)","description":"## What Implement the 'runtimes' category for acfs-update: - Bun: bun upgrade - Rust: rustup update (stable, nightly if installed) - uv: uv self update (or reinstall via official installer) - Go: apt handles if system-installed, skip otherwise ## Technical Details - Each runtime has different update mechanisms - Bun: ~/.bun/bin/bun upgrade - Rust: ~/.cargo/bin/rustup update - uv: May need to source the installer again - Go: Typically managed via apt, but could be manual install ## Considerations - Must run as target user, not root (except go via apt) - Capture before/after versions for each - Skip if tool not installed (detected by missing binary) - Rust may update multiple toolchains - capture all ## Success Criteria - [ ] Each runtime updates correctly - [ ] Skips uninstalled runtimes gracefully - [ ] Version changes logged - [ ] Works for both root and non-root users","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:07.808375-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:50.956617-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:50.956617-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented version tracking for bun/rust/uv, added update_go() function","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3mp","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:24.85834-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3nk","title":"Silence Next.js turbopack root warning in monorepo","description":"Next.js build warns about inferred Turbopack workspace root because there are multiple bun.lock files (repo root + apps/web). Configure apps/web/next.config.ts turbopack.root explicitly so Next/Turbopack uses the intended root and the warning goes away (no file deletions).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:30:19.912859-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:34:12.38145-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:34:12.38145-05:00","close_reason":"Set apps/web turbopack.root to the workspace root so Next/Turbopack resolves deps correctly and the monorepo root warning is silenced without deleting any lockfiles.","labels":["dx","web"]}
